_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 3-3 Respiratory Distress (Revised 08/11/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured. For patients < 14 years refer to Pediatric Respiratory Distress protocol.
protocol_reference: Michigan Adult Treatment Protocol Section 3-3: Respiratory Distress — MDHHS Approved 8/11/23, Reviewed 2023
purpose: For patients < 14 years refer to Pediatric Respiratory Distress-Treatment Protocol.

sections.title: General Assessment
points: Follow General Pre-hospital Care-Treatment Protocol
Allow patient a position of comfort
Determine the type of respiratory problem involved
Crackles of suspected cardiac etiology or fluid overload: refer to Pulmonary Edema/Cardiogenic Shock-Treatment Protocol

sections.title: Clear Breath Sounds
points: Consider: possible metabolic problems, MI, pulmonary embolus, hyperventilation
Obtain 12-lead ECG (per MCA selection, may be a BLS or Specialist procedure) per 12 Lead ECG-Procedure Protocol

sections.title: Asymmetrical Breath Sounds
points: If evidence of tension pneumothorax and patient is unstable: consider decompression — refer to Pleural Decompression-Procedure Protocol (Specialist skill)

sections.title: Stridor / Upper Airway Obstruction
points: Complete obstruction: follow Foreign Body Airway Obstruction-Treatment Protocol

Partial obstruction (epiglottitis, foreign body, anaphylaxis, etc.): follow Airway Management-Procedure Protocol; consider anaphylaxis — see Anaphylaxis/Allergic Reaction-Treatment Protocol; transport in position of comfort

sections.title: Rhonchi (Suspected Pneumonia)
points: Sit patient upright
Consider CPAP per CPAP-Procedure Protocol (Specialist skill)
Consider NS or LR IV/IO fluid bolus up to 1 liter wide open if tachycardia — repeat as needed per Vascular Access and IV Fluid Therapy-Procedure Protocol (Specialist skill)

sections.title: Crackles
points: Crackles of suspected non-cardiac etiology/fluid: follow wheezing/diminished breath sound management below
Crackles of suspected cardiac etiology/CHF/cardiogenic shock: refer to Pulmonary Edema/Cardiogenic Shock-Treatment Protocol

sections.title: Wheezing / Diminished Breath Sounds (Asthma, COPD)
points: Assist the patient in using their own albuterol inhaler if available
Administer albuterol 2.5 mg/3 mL NS nebulized (per MCA selection, may be EMT skill) per Medication Administration-Medication Protocol
Consider CPAP per CPAP-Procedure Protocol (Specialist skill)
Administer epinephrine auto-injector (0.3 mg) IM in patients with impending respiratory failure unable to tolerate nebulizer therapy (per MCA selection, may be MFR skill)
NOTE: BLS not carrying epinephrine auto-injector MUST participate in draw-up epinephrine
Administer epinephrine 1 mg/mL, 0.3 mg (0.3 mL) IM in patients with impending respiratory failure unable to tolerate nebulizer therapy (per MCA selection, may be BLS or MFR skill)

Administer nebulized albuterol 2.5 mg/3 mL NS and ipratropium 500 mcg/2.5 mL NS if wheezing and/or airway constriction per Medication Administration-Medication Protocol (per MCA selection, may be Specialist skill)
Administer prednisone tablet 50 mg PO to adults and children > 6 years of age (if available per MCA selection)

If prednisone is not available, patient is < 6 years of age, or patient is unable to receive medication PO: administer methylprednisolone IV/IO/IM — Adults: 125 mg; Pediatrics: 2 mg/kg (max 125 mg) (Specialist skill)
Contact Medical Control and consider repeat epinephrine 1 mg/mL, 0.3 mg (0.3 mL) IM in asthma patients with impending respiratory failure if unable to tolerate nebulizer therapy
Consider magnesium sulfate 2 gm slow IV in refractory status asthmaticus — administer by adding 2 gm to 100–250 mL NS and infusing over approximately 10 minutes (Specialist skill)

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may authorize EMT to administer nebulized albuterol 2.5 mg/3 mL NS
MCA may authorize MFR to administer epinephrine auto-injector (0.3 mg) IM for impending respiratory failure. MCA responsible for maintaining roster of participating agencies submitted to MDHHS.

MCA may authorize MFR and/or BLS to administer drawn-up epinephrine 1 mg/mL, 0.3 mg IM. Personnel must complete MCA-approved training prior to participating. MCA responsible for maintaining roster submitted to MDHHS.
MCA may authorize Specialist to administer combined nebulized albuterol/ipratropium
MCA may include prednisone 50 mg tablet PO as an additional medication option (adults and children > 6 years)
MCA must select whether 12-lead ECG is a BLS or Specialist procedure

key_drugs.name: Albuterol Nebulized (2.5 mg/3 mL NS)
key_drugs.dose: 2.5 mg/3 mL NS via nebulizer; may repeat
key_drugs.side_effects: Tachycardia, tremor, hypokalemia with repeated dosing
key_drugs.scope: Per MCA selection — EMT option available
key_drugs.notes: Assist patient with own MDI first if available.

key_drugs.name: Albuterol/Ipratropium Nebulized (Combined)
key_drugs.dose: Albuterol 2.5 mg/3 mL NS + Ipratropium 500 mcg/2.5 mL NS combined
key_drugs.contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to either agent; use caution in glaucoma, BPH
key_drugs.notes: Combined dual bronchodilator therapy for moderate-severe bronchospasm.

key_drugs.name: Epinephrine Auto-Injector (0.3 mg) IM
key_drugs.indication: Impending respiratory failure with inability to tolerate nebulizer therapy
key_drugs.contraindications: Use with caution in patients > 65 or with cardiac history — weigh risk vs. benefit in life-threatening bronchospasm
key_drugs.scope: Per MCA selection — MFR option available
key_drugs.notes: BLS not carrying auto-injector MUST participate in draw-up epinephrine procedure.

key_drugs.name: Epinephrine 1 mg/mL, 0.3 mg (0.3 mL) IM (Draw-Up)
key_drugs.indication: Impending respiratory failure unable to tolerate nebulizer therapy
key_drugs.contraindications: Use with caution in elderly and cardiac patients — benefit outweighs risk in life-threatening bronchospasm
key_drugs.scope: Per MCA selection — BLS or MFR option available; requires MCA-approved training
key_drugs.notes: Contact Medical Control and consider repeat dose in asthma patients with impending respiratory failure. Must complete MCA-approved draw-up training.

key_drugs.name: Prednisone 50 mg PO
key_drugs.dose: 50 mg PO — adults and children > 6 years
key_drugs.indication: Bronchospasm — systemic corticosteroid for anti-inflammatory effect
key_drugs.contraindications: Patient unable to swallow; patient < 6 years; known hypersensitivity
key_drugs.side_effects: Hyperglycemia, GI upset (minimal with single dose)
key_drugs.scope: Per MCA selection — may be Specialist skill
key_drugs.notes: If unavailable or patient cannot take PO: substitute methylprednisolone IV/IO/IM.

key_drugs.name: Methylprednisolone IV/IO/IM
key_drugs.indication: Bronchospasm when prednisone unavailable, patient < 6 years, or unable to take PO
key_drugs.notes: Parenteral corticosteroid alternative when oral prednisone is not feasible.

key_drugs.name: Magnesium Sulfate (Refractory Status Asthmaticus)
key_drugs.dose: 2 gm slow IV — add to 100–250 mL NS, infuse over approximately 10 minutes
key_drugs.indication: Refractory status asthmaticus not responding to bronchodilators
key_drugs.side_effects: Flushing, hypotension, bradycardia if administered too rapidly
key_drugs.notes: Dilute before administration. Infuse slowly. Contact Medical Control before use in refractory asthma.

deterioration_flags: Impending respiratory failure (unable to speak full sentences, tripod positioning, accessory muscle use, SpO2 < 90%) — epinephrine IM if unable to tolerate nebulizer; advanced airway preparation; contact Medical Control
Tension pneumothorax signs (absent breath sounds, tracheal deviation, JVD, hypotension) — needle decompression (Specialist skill) per Pleural Decompression protocol
Status asthmaticus unresponsive to nebulized therapy — magnesium sulfate (Specialist skill); contact Medical Control; expedite transport
Cardiac crackles suspected — switch to Pulmonary Edema/Cardiogenic Shock protocol
scope_notes: Position of comfort, own MDI assistance, oxygen: all levels
Albuterol nebulized: per MCA selection — EMT option available
Epinephrine auto-injector IM: per MCA selection — MFR option available
Draw-up epinephrine IM: per MCA selection — MFR/BLS option with MCA-approved training
CPAP: Specialist skill in base Michigan protocol — EMT scope at MCAs with cpap_bls expansion
Albuterol/ipratropium combined nebulized: Specialist skill — per MCA selection
Methylprednisolone IV/IM, magnesium sulfate: Specialist skill
12-lead ECG: per MCA selection — BLS or Specialist
